sections.paragraph:
συμφύω 2 aor. pass. ptc. συμφυείς (trans. in Pla. et al.; intr. in Hippocr., Pla. et al., incl. Wsd 13:13; Philo, Dec. 87; Jos., Bell. 6, 155, Ant. 8, 63 [συμφυέντες]) pass. intr. w. act. force grow up with someth. Lk 8:7.—DELG s.v. φύομαι C. M-M. s.v. συνφύω. Spicq.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

trans. trans. = transitive

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

intr. intr. = intransitive

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

incl. incl. = including

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

act. act. = active

someth. someth. = something

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 960
